status_label: Truce Holding — 10% Reciprocal Levy Remains; Section 301 Pending
status_note: US-China ag trade remains under the Oct 30, 2025 Busan truce (reciprocal-tariff suspension extended through Nov 10, 2026). China TERMINATED its March 2025 ag-specific retaliatory tariffs (15% chicken/wheat/corn/cotton; 10% soybeans/pork/sorghum/etc.) effective Nov 10, 2025, AND suspended the 24% additional tariff — but RETAINED a 10% reciprocal levy on all US goods. Net effect: US grain/oilseed/pork still face MFN + 10% (e.g., soybeans 13%, per Reuters). Section 301 investigations (excess capacity + forced labor, opened Mar 17, 2026) are pending, with a USTR tariff decision targeted around July 24, 2026 — a live re-escalation risk. On May 6, 2026 USTR also opened the second four-year review of the 2018 Section 301 tariffs (comments May 7–Jul 5, 2026). VERIFY in-quota/out-of-quota (TRQ) treatment and any product-specific exclusions against USDA FAS GAIN and the Federal Register before any trading decision.
headline: China ag-tariff truce holding post-Busan, but a 10% reciprocal levy remains (soybeans 13%) and Section 301 investigations pending a ~July 24 decision keep re-escalation on the table.

rows.note: 3% MFN + 10% retained reciprocal levy. The March 2025 retaliatory 10% was terminated Nov 10, 2025 under the Busan truce, but China kept a 10% levy on all US goods; Reuters reports soybeans remain at 13%. Section 301 (Apr 28 hearings) could re-escalate. Verify TRQ/exclusions via USDA FAS GAIN.

1% in-quota MFN + 10% retained reciprocal levy. The March 2025 retaliatory 15% was terminated Nov 10, 2025; the 10% all-goods levy remains. Out-of-quota corn carries a much higher TRQ rate (~65%) before the 10% — confirm your quota status. China corn purchases remain minimal. Verify via USDA FAS GAIN.

1% in-quota MFN + 10% retained reciprocal levy. March 2025 retaliatory 15% terminated Nov 10, 2025; 10% all-goods levy remains. Out-of-quota wheat carries a much higher TRQ rate (~65%) before the 10%. Minimal China wheat demand. Verify via USDA FAS GAIN.

~12% MFN + 10% retained reciprocal levy. March 2025 retaliatory 10% terminated Nov 10, 2025; 10% all-goods levy remains. China remains a major US pork/offal market. Confirm the exact MFN base and any exclusions via USDA FAS GAIN.
USMCA-compliant, duty-free. USMCA exemption share reached ~86% of Canada/Mexico imports by early 2026. Soybean groups have urged USTR to exempt Canada/Mexico from any Section 301 remedies.
USMCA-compliant, duty-free. Mexico remains the top US corn export destination; Canadian demand steady.

EU anti-dumping duty on US undenatured ethanol in place since 2013 (€83.03/metric ton base); some member states apply higher max rates up to €192/m³. UK-US deal (May 2025) opened a 1.4B L/yr duty-free quota for the UK only. Verify member-state customs for specific shipments.

timeline.event: USTR opens its second statutory four-year review of the 2018 Section 301 tariffs on Chinese imports; domestic-industry comment window May 7–Jul 5, 2026.
USTR Section 301 hearings (excess capacity + forced labor) held; tariff decision targeted around July 24, 2026 — soybean groups warn remedies could trigger renewed Chinese retaliation.
USTR initiates Section 301 investigations into China and other partners over 'excess capacity' and 'forced labor.'
Section 122 global 10% tariff takes effect (150-day measure) after IEEPA tariffs are terminated.
US Supreme Court rules IEEPA does not authorize tariff imposition; April 2025 reciprocal tariffs voided; CBP refund process begins.

China terminates its March 2025 ag-specific retaliatory tariffs (15% chicken/wheat/corn/cotton; 10% soybeans/pork/etc.) and suspends the 24% additional tariff — but RETAINS a 10% reciprocal levy on all US goods (soybeans remain at 13%).
Trump-Xi meeting in Busan: reciprocal-tariff suspension extended through Nov 10, 2026; China commits to 25 MMT/yr US soybean purchases 2026–2028 plus $17B/yr other ag.
China imposes first-tranche retaliatory tariffs on US ag (15% wheat/corn/cotton/chicken; 10% soybeans/pork/etc.) — later terminated under the Busan truce.
